Introduction
We propose a flow matching framework for protein sequence design. The goal is to improve controllable biological sequence generation with stronger representation learning.
Method
Our model combines a protein language model backbone with flow matching objectives and sequence-level constraints.
Experiments
We evaluate on multiple protein family design benchmarks, compare against autoregressive and diffusion baselines, and report stronger sequence fitness with ablations.
Conclusion
The method is useful when researchers want targeted protein design instead of generic sequence generation.
